appendix.title: Third-Party Data Breach — ACME Technology Incident Report

sections.summary: In early March 2026, a data breach was reported involving a client's independently managed internal system. The breach exposed approximately 33,000 records of supporter personal data on an underground forum. Our company's donation platform was mentioned in the media because the leaked data contained a field referencing our payment service. After thorough investigation, we confirmed our systems were not breached.
sections.relation: Media reports noted that leaked data contained a reference to our payment service. As the client's donation platform provider, public concern briefly associated our system with the incident.
sections.relation_conclusion: After comprehensive investigation and technical forensics, we confirmed the incident occurred in the client's self-built internal system and is completely unrelated to our cloud platform.
relation_details: Completely independent system architecture: Our platform and the client's internal system are entirely separate, with no shared databases or access.
The reference to our service was a label created by the client in their own CRM system to mark the donation source — it was not data generated by our system.
Different database technologies: The leaked data was in MySQL/SQL format, while our system uses NoSQL (DynamoDB), further proving the data did not originate from our system.
timeline: Media report published revealing the client's data breach
Completed full system security scan and access log review
Confirmed our system was unaffected, published public statement
Conducted deep system audit, prepared this report
conclusions: Incident attribution confirmed: The breach occurred in the client's self-built internal system, completely unrelated to our cloud platform.
System security confirmed: Full system audit found no anomalous access or intrusion indicators. All security monitoring metrics are normal.
Independent architecture: Our system and the client's system are completely independent with no data exchange paths.
follow_up: Continuous monitoring: Maintaining 24/7 threat detection mechanisms.
Notification completed: Internal incident notification procedures completed.
Client advisory: Recommended the client conduct a security forensics investigation and notify affected individuals per the Personal Data Protection Act.
Enhanced client data governance: Evaluating additional data governance clauses in service agreements.
